Internal Incident Report — Confidential under EU MAR and GDPR; Issuer: Virelia Group SE, listed on Alpenex Main Market (EU)
Prepared by Dr. Marta Kellen, Chief Risk Officer; timestamp: 06 June 2026, 11:20 CET

Summary: A spear‑phishing campaign coupled with a compromised third‑party API resulted in exfiltration of a limited HR subfolder and an operations snapshot from Virelia Digital Assets Sàrl; files included special category indicators (GDPR Art 9: union membership flags) and crypto custody access logs.
The breach also exposed draft CSRD climate KPIs (unpublished Scope 1–3 figures and transition plan assumptions) and a pre‑decision SFDR reclassification note for Virelia Green Yield Sub‑Fund (proposed shift from Art 8 to enhanced Art 8+), none of which are public.
Materiality (MAR): preliminary assessment indicates potential impact on Q3 DPT custody volumes and exceptional remediation opex of €1.2–1.6m; the information is precise, non‑public, and likely price‑sensitive.
Actions taken: insider list updated at 09:30 CET for “Project Glacier”; trading blackout imposed for all insiders through the pre‑close period; information barriers tightened between Digital Assets and Sustainability units; all analyst enquiries to be routed to IR to avoid tipping.
Cross‑border transfer: a backup dataset was held by processor Novanica Pte Ltd in Auriga (non‑EEA); processing is covered by EU Standard Contractual Clauses with a documented transfer impact assessment and supplemental encryption; transfers suspended pending DPO consultation with the Nordland DPA.
Data subjects: 12 employees and 4 contractors affected; elements include emails, payroll IDs, and hashed occupational health survey flags; no national identifiers or bank account numbers present.
Crypto operations: HSM‑protected hot‑wallet keys remain intact; no anomalous on‑chain movements detected; key rotation scheduled and custodial segregation validated; client DPT balances unaffected.
Commercial terms exposure: a draft fee schedule and volume rebate bands for an unannounced OTC venue partnership with QuadraX OTC were in the compromised folder; these deal‑stage commercial terms are non‑public and could influence valuation.
Selective disclosure controls: deal‑room access restricted to named insiders; no dissemination outside the insider list; all external vendors placed on need‑to‑know with NDAs refreshed; compliance monitoring for inadvertent signalling on social channels enabled.
ESG/climate note: the exposed CSRD metrics include a preliminary 18% YoY Scope 2 reduction tied to a yet‑to‑be‑executed PPA; figures are subject to assurance and internal audit and should not be relied upon by third parties.
Educational/ops‑only: the “Insider Lists and Market Soundings 101” staff webinar will proceed using anonymised training materials and will not reference Project Glacier, crypto custody, or CSRD metrics.
Regulatory next steps: maintain delay of disclosure register under MAR Art 17; prepare draft ad hoc announcement for board approval if delay no longer justified; GDPR Art 33 notification to the Nordland DPA within 72h; data subject notices in EU languages to follow.
IR/comms: pre‑close Q2 blackout remains in force; no comments to media beyond approved holding lines; any sustainability queries to be handled centrally to avoid selective disclosure of CSRD/SFDR positions.
Contact: Mr. Oskar Jantzen (DPO) dpo@virelia.eu; Ms. Lene Duarte (Head of Compliance) compliance@virelia.eu; emergency bridge details available on the MAR portal.
Note: This document contains non‑public operational details and ESG metrics; onward sharing is prohibited except to the “Project Glacier” insider list (ref. A‑LI‑2026‑061).
